logs.heading: A0: Monitoring Jira sync progress
logs.content:
{
    "thoughts": [
        "Jira sync started with PID 2588 in background",
        "Now I need to monitor the log file up to 10 times, checking every 30 seconds",
        "I'll create a monitoring loop that checks if the process is still running and reads the last 5 lines of the log",
        "Once complete or fails, send appropriate notification"
    ],
    "headline": "Monitoring Jira sync progress",
    "tool_name": "code_execution_tool",
    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "terminal",
        "session": 0,
        "reset": false,
        "code": "#!/bin/bash
LOG_FILE="/a0/usr/workdir/jira-debug.log"
MAX_CHECKS=10
CHECK_INTERVAL=30

for i in $(seq 1 $MAX_CHECKS); do
    sleep $CHECK_INTERVAL
    
    # Check if process is still running
    if ! ps -p 2588 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Process completed or failed"
        break
    fi
    
    echo "Check $i: Process still running..."
    tail -5 "$LOG_FILE" || echo "Log file not yet available"
done

echo "Monitoring complete. Final status:"
if ps -p 2588 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "❌ Process still running after $MAX_CHECKS checks - timeout"
elif [ ! -f "$LOG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "❌ Log file not found"
else
    tail -5 "$LOG_FILE"
fi
"
    }
}

I'll create a monitoring loop that checks if the process is still running and reads the last 5 lines of the log
Once complete or fails, send appropriate notification

kvps.code:
#!/bin/bash
LOG_FILE="/a0/usr/workdir/jira-debug.log"
MAX_CHECKS=10
CHECK_INTERVAL=30

for i in $(seq 1 $MAX_CHECKS); do
    sleep $CHECK_INTERVAL
    
    # Check if process is still running
    if ! ps -p 2588 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
        echo "Process completed or failed"
        break
    fi
    
    echo "Check $i: Process still running..."
    tail -5 "$LOG_FILE" || echo "Log file not yet available"
done

echo "Monitoring complete. Final status:"
if ps -p 2588 > /dev/null 2>&1; then
    echo "❌ Process still running after $MAX_CHECKS checks - timeout"
elif [ ! -f "$LOG_FILE" ]; then
    echo "❌ Log file not found"
else
    tail -5 "$LOG_FILE"
fi

logs.heading: A0: Checking Jira sync completion status

thoughts: The monitoring script started and the Jira sync process is running in background
I can see the log file exists now, so I should check its current status
Let me check if the process completed by examining the last lines of the debug log
If complete, count tickets cached and send success notification
kvps.headline: Checking Jira sync completion status

logs.heading: A0: Checking Jira sync results and sending notification
logs.content:
{
    "thoughts": [
        "The Jira sync process appears to have completed - I can see jira-output.md exists",
        "I need to check the debug log for completion status and count the tickets cached",
        "Then send the appropriate notification based on success or failure"
    ],
    "headline": "Checking Jira sync results and sending notification",
    "tool_name": "code_execution_tool",
    "tool_args": {
        "runtime": "terminal",
        "session": 0,
        "reset": false,
        "code": "echo "=== Jira Sync Final Status ==="
echo "Last log entries:"
tail -15 /a0/usr/workdir/jira-debug.log
echo ""
echo "Output file exists: $(test -f /a0/usr/workdir/jira-output.md && echo 'YES' || echo 'NO')"

# Count tickets in output
if [ -f /a0/usr/workdir/jira-output.md ]; then
    TICKET_COUNT=$(grep -c '^\[.*\]' /a0/usr/workdir/jira-output.md || echo "0")
    echo "Tickets cached: $TICKET_COUNT"
else
    # Check for error in log
    if grep -qi 'error\|failed\|exception' /a0/usr/workdir/jira-debug.log; then
        ERROR_MSG=$(grep -i 'error\|failed\|exception' /a0/usr/workdir/jira-debug.log | tail -1)
        echo "Error detected: $ERROR_MSG"
    fi
fi
"
    }
}
